claim: TinyImageNet Toric/PJ and mixed-r4 heads store usable local geometry in early layers, and the early local pool is causally sufficient and necessary.
primary_evidence: C6 seed626: Toric/PJ L0-L3 keep 0.8475 vs full 0.8279; ablate 0.3037 near zero 0.2790. Mixed r4 L0-L3 keep 0.8665 vs full 0.8521; ablate 0.2698 near zero 0.2484.
supporting_metrics: early_pool_keep_minus_full: Toric/PJ +0.0197, Mixed +0.0144; early_pool_ablate_minus_zero: Toric/PJ +0.0248, Mixed +0.0214

claim: Toric/PJ full-score failure is mainly boundary/far-shell leakage, not visible-region inability.
primary_evidence: TinyImageNet Toric/PJ 10k visible 0.8626 and r<=4 0.8628, but full 0.8255 and far band 0.1200; r6-9 energy 0.6417 with r<=4 energy 0.1477.

claim: Mixed residual-DCT32 plus hard train-radial r4 redirects energy into the local chart and partially repairs extrapolation.
primary_evidence: TinyImageNet mixed hard r4 10k final 0.8449, visible 0.8658, far band 0.2565; r<=4 energy 0.7784 and r6-9 energy 0.1332.
supporting_metrics: vs Toric/PJ: final +0.0194, full-visible gap improves from -0.0371 to -0.0209, far band improves from 0.1200 to 0.2565
verdict: positive but below compact DCT ceiling

claim: Soft radial boundary w=1 is a task-level full-score improvement but not a far-shell geometry improvement.
primary_evidence: TinyImageNet soft w=1 10k final 0.850741 vs hard r4 0.844904; full-visible gap -0.013673 vs -0.020903; far band 0.233884 vs hard 0.256508.
supporting_metrics: soft w=1 seed finals 0.8524/0.8447/0.8551; heldout clamp 0.8632 vs hard 0.8477
verdict: split verdict: full-score pass, far-shell gate fail

claim: Learned mask-chart tuning is not currently the right path to far-shell generalization.
primary_evidence: Smooth+monotonic learned chart regularizes the mask, but final 0.676945 and far band 0.214728 remain below fixed soft w=1 2k and hard r4 2k baselines.
supporting_metrics: negative gates: radial-only, radial-angular a8, quadrant-tied a4, smooth+monotonic all fail task/far-band gate
verdict: negative for mask-weight tuning; next attempt should change parameterization/objective

claim: Simple alternatives are ruled out: larger train radius, scalar far-tail penalty, direct LC/log warp, and structural compression do not explain the successful mechanism.
primary_evidence: r5 collapses full score; tail penalties worsen full-visible gap; LC/log coordinate warp is visible-good but full-bad; top16 compression is destructive.
supporting_metrics: see boundary policy appendix rows top32/top64 r5, tail1/tail10, lc/log, top16

claim: Cross-dataset diagnostics support the local-chart concentration vs far-shell leakage explanation.
primary_evidence: CIFAR100/CIFAR10/SVHN mixed variants show higher local energy and smaller full-visible gaps than Toric/PJ leakage cases; STL10 recovers when train-radial r4 is used.
supporting_metrics: see v8_cross_dataset_theory_table.csv for energy_r_le_4, energy_r_6_9, radial_band_6_9 and boundary_pollution
